caption:
4 MCP servers that turn Claude Code into an autonomous agent.

Browser control. Database access. Figma designs. File system. All from one terminal conversation.

This is what AI-assisted development looks like in 2026.

📌 SAVE this — your dev workflow is about to change.

Which MCP server would you add first? 👇

slides.title: MCP servers that turn Claude into an autonomous agent
slides.subtitle: Browser. Database. Figma. All from your terminal.

slides.title: Browser automation — Claude controls Chrome
slides.desc: Navigate pages, click buttons, fill forms, take screenshots. All via natural language.
slides.punchline: E2E testing without writing test code.

slides.title: Database access — query from conversation
slides.desc: Claude reads your schema, writes queries, and shows results. No SQL client needed.
slides.punchline: SQL from natural language. Live data.

slides.title: Figma integration — design to code
slides.desc: Claude reads your Figma designs, extracts components, and generates matching code.

slides.title: All at once — combine servers
slides.desc: Claude uses multiple MCP servers in one conversation. Read Figma, write code, test in browser, query DB.
slides.punchline: The full dev cycle. Zero context switching.
